items.title: Gait names the funnel's WORST step when it is diagnosable
steps: 1. npx vitest run src/__tests__/diagnostic-gait.test.ts -t 'names the worst step'
items.expected: On an 8000 -> 4000 -> 200 funnel the leak is 'leads', exactly one conversion carries isLeak, and basis is not 'abstained'.
items.evidence: 3 test files, 70 tests passed on Node 22.

items.title: Feature-vs-bug: click-through is not blamed when no top query reaches page one
steps: 1. npx vitest run src/__tests__/diagnostic-gait.test.ts -t 'first page'
items.expected: The impressions->visits step is excluded with excludedBy 'expected-for-position' when top10QueryCount is 0, and the gait ABSTAINS rather than promoting the healthier step.
items.evidence: Passed. Also covers the inverse: with top10QueryCount=5 and an average position of 14.7 the step stays diagnosable, which an average-position gate would have wrongly excluded.

items.title: Sample floor: a 12.5% rate on 8 visits is not diagnosed
steps: 1. npx vitest run src/__tests__/diagnostic-gait.test.ts -t 'too small'
items.expected: Step excluded with 'insufficient-sample'; because it is the funnel's worst step, the gait abstains rather than naming the healthier survivor.
items.evidence: Passed. The healthier 40% click-through step does not inherit the verdict.

items.title: Move 5: the gait abstains and reports the driving finding
steps: 1. npx vitest run src/__tests__/diagnostic-gait.test.ts -t 'abstains'
items.expected: leakStageKey null, basis 'abstained', abstainedBecause is the real finding ('expected-for-position') rather than plain 'no-data', and no conversion carries isLeak.
items.evidence: Passed. abstainedBecause reports the exclusion that applied to the WORST step, not a ranked guess.
items.notes: This is the behaviour the replaced code could not express at all.

items.title: Regression guard: excluding the worst step never promotes a healthy one (and never emits a false bookable card)
steps: 1. npx vitest run src/__tests__/diagnostic-gait.test.ts -t 'NEVER promotes'
2. npx vitest run src/__tests__/diagnostic-gait.test.ts -t 'bookable card off a promoted'
items.expected: On impressions=8000 (top10QueryCount 0) / visits=80 / leads=40, the gait abstains: leakStageKey null, no conversion flagged isLeak, and buildBookableCandidate returns null.
items.evidence: Passed. This is the defect an independent cross-model adversary proved against the first draft: the healthy 50% step was named 'your largest opportunity' and the bookable card told the owner the booking step was 'where you're losing the most' while 7,920 people were lost upstream.
items.notes: The first draft's own test suite asserted the wrong answer as correct.

items.title: GSC position 0 is a no-data sentinel, not a first-place ranking
steps: 1. npx vitest run src/__tests__/diagnostic-gait.test.ts -t 'position 0'
items.expected: With gsc {position:0, ctr:0}, the step is neither excluded as 'expected-for-position' nor given any corroboration string claiming first-page rank.
items.evidence: Passed. summarizeGsc (stageReaders.ts:182-185) returns position 0 when organic impressions are 0, which a maps-only account hits while still carrying a large stage value.

items.title: Contract shape is unchanged — pre-existing suite passes unmodified
steps: 1. npx vitest run src/__tests__/patient-journey.service.test.ts
items.expected: All pre-existing assertions pass with no edits to the test file, including the leak-selection and empty-state headline tests.
items.evidence: Passed unmodified. The no-data headline string is byte-identical.

items.title: TypeScript build gate (backend + frontend)
steps: 1. npx tsc --noEmit
2. cd frontend && npx tsc --noEmit
items.evidence: Both exit 0, no output. The only frontend change is a corrected doc comment on the isLeak mirror.

items.title: Code Constitution structural check
steps: 1. npm run check:conventions --strict
items.expected: Zero clear structural violations for backend (size + console + db).
items.evidence: CLEAR structural violations — backend: 0. Advisory Tier A and VOCAB-BYPASS items are pre-existing and touch none of the files changed here.

items.title: Landing gate: reasoning visible on a real account on dev
items.precondition: PR merged by Dave and deployed to dev.getalloro.com
steps: 1. Call GET /api/patient-journey for a real connected account
2. Confirm the response shape matches the previous contract exactly
3. Read the '[patient-journey] assembled funnel' log line for that request
items.expected: Payload shape unchanged; the log line carries diagnosisBasis, per-step pct/denominator/eligible/excludedBy and any corroboration strings.
items.notes: Blocked on Dave merging and deploying. This is the step that converts 'written' into 'landed'.

items.title: Landing gate: runs unattended AND the abstention rate is confirmed acceptable
steps: 1. Let a full assembly cycle run across connected accounts
2. From the '[patient-journey] assembled funnel' logs, count assemblies where basis == 'abstained', broken down by abstainedBecause
3. Compare against the accounts that a human agrees were genuinely undiagnosable
items.expected: No errors across the cycle, AND the abstention rate is acceptable — the gait names a constraint on accounts that genuinely have a diagnosable one. A coordinator that abstains on most accounts is a dead feature, not a safe one. If abstentions are dominated by 'insufficient-sample' or 'expected-for-position' on accounts a human would diagnose, the gates are mis-set and this brick needs revision before brick 2 builds on it.
items.notes: Nobody has run this on production data. The abstention rate is UNKNOWN, not low. This item is a pass criterion, not an observation: 'it runs' does not close it.

items.title: Dashboard: a healthy-data account shows a diagnosed leak, and never blames a healthy step
items.precondition: PR merged and deployed to dev.getalloro.com; a real connected account with real funnel traffic (use the seeded Pilot flow into One Endodontics per AGENTS.md).
steps: 1. Log into https://dev.getalloro.com and open the client dashboard for One Endodontics (or admin-impersonate into it).
2. Open Patient Journey Insights and read the opportunity headline.
3. Note which step is flagged as the leak (the highlighted conversion / 'Your largest opportunity is moving people from X to Y') and read the surrounding step percentages.
4. Confirm the flagged step is the funnel's WORST measured step, not a higher-percentage downstream step.
items.expected: The headline names one diagnosable step as the largest opportunity (e.g. 'Your largest opportunity is moving people from Website Visitors to Website Leads. Only N% moved through.') AND the flagged step is not a healthy/high-percentage step while a worse upstream step is ignored. If this account's worst step is undiagnosable, the headline abstains instead (see T13) — it must never name a healthy step.
items.notes: Cross-check the visible verdict against the '[patient-journey] assembled funnel' log line's diagnosisBasis + per-step eligible/excludedBy for the same request. Read-only review could not confirm this account's live funnel numbers.

items.title: Dashboard: a thin-traffic account abstains instead of asserting a leak
items.precondition: PR merged and deployed to dev.getalloro.com; a connected account with thin traffic or a maps-only / newly-connected account whose worst step falls below the sample floor or has no top query on page one. Candidate accounts to check: Garrison Orthodontics, Artful Orthodontics, Caswell Orthodontics, Woodbridge — pick one whose visits are low.
steps: 1. Log into https://dev.getalloro.com and open Patient Journey Insights for the thin-traffic account.
2. Read the opportunity headline and check whether any conversion step is flagged as the leak.
items.expected: The headline shows one of the abstention strings — 'Too few people moved through your funnel last month to point to a single drop-off with any confidence.' (insufficient-sample), 'None of your top search terms are reaching the first page of Google yet, so the low click-through follows from where you rank rather than from your page titles.' (expected-for-position), or 'Connect more of your data...' (no-data) — and NO conversion step is highlighted as a confident leak. No invented 'Your largest opportunity is...' verdict on a handful of visitors.
items.notes: Confirm no step carries isLeak in the payload for this account. Read-only review could not confirm which live account currently has thin traffic; runner should pick one and record which.
